originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&codec_hw_write` is never used.

presenceCondition:
(and (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 true)
     (= CONFIG_I2C true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 true))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_I2C () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 () Bool
  true)
reasoning: No contradictions: FSI_DA7210=T AND SOC_DA7210=T consistent (FSI defines SOC internally), FSI_AK4642=T AND SOC_AK4642=T consistent. Dead store at L16 (codec_hw_write = i2c_master_send in da7210_init, never read) when SOC_DA7210=T AND I2C=T — new bug. The PC adds irrelevant FSI_DA7210=T, FSI_AK4642=T, SOC_AK4642=T constraints that do not affect da7210_init dead store. PC over-constrained (too narrow).
